evals.prompt: I've read a .fsf file and found these readable strings: form title 'Node Export Utility', controls: ProfusionHierarchyCtrl1 (CObjActiveX, ProgID ProfusionHierarchyCtrl.ProfusionHierarchyCtrl.1), chx_ExportByNode (CObjCheckbox), chx_ExportByPog (CObjCheckbox), txtOutputPath (CObjEdit), cmd_Export (CObjEdit button), CScriptManager. Produce a control inventory table and identify the OA equivalent for each control.
evals.expected_output: A markdown table with columns: DM Control Name, DM Type, Purpose, OA Equivalent. ProfusionHierarchyCtrl1 → no ASCX control, subscribe PublishedKey.HierarchyDBKeySelectedNodeList. chx_ExportByNode and chx_ExportByPog are mutual-exclusion checkboxes → asp:RadioButtonList (not two separate checkboxes). txtOutputPath → asp:TextBox. cmd_Export → ICommandManager toolbar button at CommandLocations.TopRight. CScriptManager → no OA equivalent, contains VBScript to translate.

evals.prompt:
Convert this VBScript from a DM form to the equivalent OA C# pattern:

Sub cmd_Submit_Click()
    Dim strKeys
    strKeys = ProfusionHierarchyCtrl1.Object.SelectedLeafKeys
    If strKeys = "" Then
        Application.MessageBox "Please select at least one node."
        Exit Sub
    End If
    Dim cmd
    Set cmd = CreateObject("ADODB.Command")
    cmd.ActiveConnection = Application.ProfusionSupport.ADOConnection
    cmd.CommandType = 4
    cmd.CommandText = "ckbcustom.cx_node_export_run"
    cmd.Parameters.Append cmd.CreateParameter("@node_keys", 200, 1, 4000, strKeys)
    cmd.Parameters.Append cmd.CreateParameter("@username", 200, 1, 100, Application.UserName)
    cmd.Execute
    Application.ShowStatusMsg "Export complete."
End Sub
evals.expected_output: DoCommand 'Submit' case in code-behind that: (1) reads node keys from SubscriptionManager.GetPageStoreData(this, PublishedKey.HierarchyDBKeySelectedNodeList), (2) validates keys are not null/empty and calls OnDisplayMessage with MessageEventTypes.Error if so, (3) calls a CommandFactory method RunNodeExport(string nodeKeys, string username) that uses DynamicParameters with parameters for @node_keys (DbType.String, size 4000) and @username (DbType.String, size 100), conn.Execute with CommandType.StoredProcedure on ckbcustom.cx_node_export_run, wrapped in Start/Finish logging and try/catch, (4) calls OnDisplayMessage with MessageEventTypes.Success 'Export complete.'

evals.prompt:
Convert this VBScript data read from a DM form to the equivalent OA CommandFactory method:

Sub Form_Load()
    Dim rs
    Set rs = Application.GetRecordset("SELECT sheet_name FROM ckbcustom.cx_export_sheets ORDER BY sheet_name")
    cmbSheetName.Clear
    While Not rs.EOF
        cmbSheetName.AddItem rs("sheet_name").Value
        rs.MoveNext
    Wend
End Sub
evals.expected_output: LoadUI() method in the code-behind calls a CommandFactory method GetExportSheets() that returns IEnumerable<string> (or a model). CommandFactory method uses Dapper conn.Query<string> or a stored proc (note: raw SQL becomes a stored proc — create cx_export_sheets_get). Method has Start/Finish logging and try/catch. LoadUI() populates ddlSheetName (asp:DropDownList) by binding the result, called from OnInit or OnPreRender on !IsPostBack.

evals.prompt:
A DM form's VBScript contains this block after calling a stored proc to create a new planogram copy:

Set oCmd = CreateObject("ADODB.Command")
Set oCmd.ActiveConnection = Application.ProfusionSupport.ADOConnection
oCmd.CommandType = 1    'adCmdText
oCmd.CommandText = "UPDATE ix_spc_planogram" & _
    " SET dbstatus = '" & pogDBStatus & "'" & _
    ", name = '" & newPogName & "'" & _
    ", dbuser = 'dbo'" & _
    " WHERE dbkey = " & newPogKey
oCmd.Execute

How should this be handled in the OA conversion?
evals.expected_output: The inline UPDATE SQL must NOT be passed through as raw SQL in OA — it must be moved into a stored procedure (e.g. ckbcustom.cx_pog_update_after_split). The CommandFactory method calls that proc via Dapper DynamicParameters with typed parameters for pogDBStatus, newPogName, and newPogKey. The raw string concatenation in VBScript is also a SQL injection risk and another reason to use a parameterized stored proc. The CommandFactory method follows the standard pattern: Start/Finish logging, try/catch, DynamicParameters, conn.Execute with CommandType.StoredProcedure, ConnectionCleanup.
